lostDestinations.source: Washington County HGR History; Henson began DCA service 1962 as Hagerstown Commuter/Allegheny Commuter
Search results: Air Midwest ended Hagerstown flights September 30, 2007
Sun Air International began EAS-subsidized IAD service November 2012; HGR lost EAS eligibility 2007, restored briefly
Direct Air launched Lakeland FL and Myrtle Beach SC June 2011; suspended August 2011; Direct Air Chapter 7 liquidation April 2012
Direct Air launched MYR service June 23, 2011; ceased April 2012 bankruptcy

note: HGR lost EAS eligibility Oct 1, 2007 (within 70 miles of larger airports); Allegiant Air sole remaining carrier since May 2012 with leisure Florida/SC routes

lostDestinations.source: Cumberland Airlines began Baltimore-Pittsburgh-DCA commuter service 1969; ended ~1990 after 20+ years per airlinehistory.co.uk
US Airways Express operated Cumberland-PIT via Latrobe stop; Air Midwest discontinued September 8, 2001
Pan Am Clipper Connection CBE-HGR-BWI began December 2001 with Maryland subsidy; ended July 2003 when subsidy not renewed - times-news.com

note: CBE has had zero scheduled commercial service since July 2003; US Airways Express PIT service ended Sept 8, 2001; final service was state-subsidized stop via HGR to BWI ending 2003

lostDestinations.source: Piedmont DC-3s began DCA service 1960; virginiaplaces.org notes DCA service ended mid-1990s as US Airways shed spoke routes
Chautauqua Jul 1996-Jun 2000; Air Midwest Jun 2000-Apr 2003; Colgan Apr 2003-Jul 2004 — PIT service ended when US Airways downgraded PIT hub to focus city
United Express IAD service Apr 2000-Dec 2001; Silver Airways EAS IAD service ended November 2016 per search results
ViaAir began CLT and SFB EAS routes 2016; ViaAir EAS routes ceased late 2017, replaced by SkyWest Apr 2018
ViaAir SHD-Orlando Sanford EAS service ended Nov 2017 when DOT shifted to SkyWest
Piedmont 1960 initial service included Roanoke; last Piedmont YS-11 left SHD 1981 per virginiaplaces.org

note: SHD currently EAS-dependent; SkyWest/American serves CLT and ORD under DOT EAS contract selected September 2025; SkyWest ended service Nov 2022 then was reselected

lostDestinations.source: virginiaplaces.org: Danville lost service to Greensboro (less than 50 miles away) as hub-spoke system made small-city service unviable; last scheduled service ended 1995
virginiaplaces.org: Danville lacked population base to maintain flights to Charlotte; service ended 1995 when EAS subsidy cut (exceeded $200/passenger)
virginiaplaces.org: Danville lost scheduled service to Winston-Salem area; all service ended 1995

note: DAN lost all scheduled service 1995; Piedmont served 1948-1979; Eastern served 1947-1964; EAS subsidy eliminated because DAN was within 210 miles of medium/large hub and cost exceeded $200/passenger per virginiaplaces.org

lostDestinations.source: flysbyairport.com Airport History: Washington DC service was on and off until 2008
flysbyairport.com Airport History: Baltimore service stopped in late 2009
flysbyairport.com: United Express began 1989 commuter service, discontinued after 8 months; Piedmont/USAir Express also served PHL at various periods

detail: Henson Airways founded SBY service in 1968 under Allegheny Commuter contract; US Airways purchased Henson in 1993 and rebranded as Piedmont; airport lost DCA service by 2008 and BWI service by late 2009, now served only by American (CLT, PHL) and Breeze Airways (leisure routes).
source: flysbyairport.com Airport History page; search results

lostDestinations.source: Search results: US Airways introduced 3 daily ILG-LGA flights following community lobbying; Delta ended ILG service September 6, 2007
Delta Connection (Atlantic Southeast) began ILG-ATL service June 29, 2006 with CRJ-200s; Delta ended ILG service September 6, 2007
Shuttle America served ILG to Hartford/BDL, Buffalo BUF, and Norfolk ORF from November 1998 until February 2000
Shuttle America ILG-BUF service ended February 2000
Shuttle America ILG-ORF service ended February 2000

detail: Delaware was the only US state without any scheduled commercial airline service during five separate periods since 1990 (including 1993-1998, 2000-2006, April 2008-June 2013, April 2015-February 2021, and June 2022-January 2023); Avelo Airlines launched base at ILG in February 2023 and now serves 8+ leisure destinations.
source: Search results citing Wikipedia Wilmington Airport (Delaware) article; DRBA press releases

lostDestinations.source: virginiaplaces.org/transportation/phf.html: AirTran started jet service PHF-ATL 1995; ceased March 9, 2012 due to Southwest Airlines merger
Search results: Allegiant provided PHF-MCO seasonal service October 2012-2014 to partially replace AirTran
virginiaplaces.org: US Airways Express served PHF to multiple hubs; service reduced significantly post-9/11 2001
virginiaplaces.org PHF history: US Airways served PHF to Philadelphia hub; service ended ~2002 after 9/11 cutbacks

detail: AirTran was the dominant carrier at PHF carrying ~50% of passengers; its March 2012 exit (due to Southwest merger) caused an 85%+ drop in annual passengers from over 1 million (2005-2011) to under 650,000 in 2012, and down to 144,966 by 2023; as of April 2026 only American Airlines CLT route remains.

lostDestinations.source: wset.com: Delta departed Lynchburg at beginning of 2011 ending LYH-ATL service; airport had its best year in 2010 with 185,000+ passengers
virginiaplaces.org/transportation/airlynchburg.html: US Airways flights to Pittsburgh stopped after 9/11 attacks
Search results: United stationed at LYH until 2002 serving IAD; United Express service ended ~2002
virginiaplaces.org: Piedmont and later US Airways Express served LYH-DCA; connections eliminated as US Airways restructured 1990s

detail: Lynchburg peaked at 185,000 annual passengers in 2010; Delta's 2011 exit left only American (CLT) for years; by June 2023 passenger traffic had recovered to ~85% of 2019 pre-pandemic levels; United Express restored ORD/IAD service by 2025.

lostDestinations.source: flyroa.com/airport-history and academic.ru Roanoke Regional: US Airways Express eliminated non-stop DCA service after 1990
flyroa.com: US Airways Express eliminated non-stop PIT service post-1990
flyroa.com: US Airways Express eliminated BWI non-stop service since 1990
flyroa.com: Continental Express began ROA-EWR January 1996; pulled out November 1, 1997
flyroa.com: US Airways Express eliminated Dayton non-stop service since 1990
flyroa.com: American Eagle ended service to its RDU hub in December 1994

detail: Roanoke was a Piedmont Airlines hub in the 1970s with 45 daily flights; after the Piedmont-USAir merger (final Piedmont ROA flight August 4, 1989) the airport steadily lost nonstop spoke connections; today ROA retains service from American, Delta, and United but to far fewer destinations than its hub-era peak.
source: flyroa.com/airport-history; search results citing Roanoke-Blacksburg Regional Airport Wikipedia

lostDestinations.source: virginiaplaces.org/transportation/aircancelled.html: Piedmont Airlines granted permission to serve HSP 1964 as intermediate stop between Staunton (SHD) and Lynchburg; last service 1993
virginiaplaces.org: Hot Springs was intermediate stop on SHD-HSP-LYH route; all HSP service ended 1993
virginiaplaces.org: Piedmont served Hot Springs connecting to DCA corridor; service ended 1993 when EAS subsidy cut as cost exceeded $200/passenger

detail: Piedmont Airlines granted CAB authority to serve HSP in 1964 targeting Homestead Resort travelers; shifted to seasonal service by 1975; EAS subsidy eliminated by DOT because HSP was within 210 miles of a medium/large hub and per-passenger cost exceeded $200 — same determination that ended Danville EAS per virginiaplaces.org.
